concepts.title: Power Factor
concepts.conceptBody:
Power in AC Circuit 

If an ac voltage $\text{V}={{\text{V}}_{0}}$ sin $\omega \text{t}$ is applied to a circuit and the resulting current is,

$I={{I}_{0}}\text{sin}\left( \omega t-\phi \right)$

with, $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{I}}_{0}}=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}}{\text{Z}}$ and $\phi =\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{X}}{\text{R}} \right)$

the instantaneous power consumed by the circuit,

$\text{P}=\text{VI}={{\text{I}}_{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{0}}\text{sin}\omega \text{tsin}\left( \omega \text{t}-\phi \right)$

So, average power consumed,

${{\text{P}}_{\text{av}}}=\frac{\mathop{\int }_{0}^{\text{T}}\text{Pdt}}{\mathop{\int }_{0}^{\text{T}}\text{dt}}=\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ T}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{0}}{{\text{I}}_{0}}\mathop{\int }_{0}^{\text{T}}\text{sin}\omega \text{tsin}\left( \omega \text{t}-\phi \right)\text{dt}$

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{P}}_{\text{av}}}=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}{{\text{I}}_{0}}}{2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ T}}\mathop{\int }_{0}^{\text{T}}\left[ \text{cos}\phi -\text{cos}\left( 2\omega \text{t}-\phi \right) \right]\text{dt}$

which on simplification with $\text{T}=\frac{2\pi }{\omega }$ gives,

${{\text{P}}_{\text{av}}}=\frac{1}{2}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{0}}{{\text{I}}_{0}}\text{cos}\phi ={{\text{V}}_{\text{rms}}}{{\text{I}}_{\text{rms}}}\text{cos}\phi $

$\text{cos}\phi $ is called 'power factor' of the circuit.

This it is clear that:

(1) In case of a 'pure resistance' as $\text{X}=0$

$\text{PF}=\text{cos}\phi =\frac{\text{R}}{\text{Z}}=\frac{\text{R}}{\text{R}}=1$

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ So, }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{P}}_{\text{av}}}={{\text{V}}_{\text{rms}}}{{\text{I}}_{\text{rms}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }1=\frac{1}{2}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{0}}{{\text{I}}_{0}}=\text{max}$

(2) In case of a pure inductance or capacitance as $R=0$

$\text{PF}=\text{cos}\phi =\frac{\text{R}}{\text{Z}}=\frac{0}{\text{X}}=0$

So, $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{P}}_{\text{av }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}}={{\text{V}}_{\text{rms }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}}{{\text{I}}_{\text{rms }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }0=0$

i.e., average power consumed per cycle in a pure inductance or capacitance is zero. So in an ac circuit if the phase difference between $\text{V}$ and $\text{I}$ is $\frac{\pi }{2}$.

i.e., if $\text{V}={{\text{V}}_{0}}\text{sin}\omega \text{t}$

$\text{I}={{\text{I}}_{0}}\text{cos}\omega \text{t}$

the power consumed by the circuit is zero. Such current is called 'wattless'.
